message:
feat(core): add differential view subscriber infrastructure and fix IN-subquery view resolution

This commit introduces the Bloodstream differential-view subscriber
system and fixes a bug where IN subqueries referencing views silently
returned NULL.

Differential view subscriber infrastructure:
- Define public types: DifferentialEvent (Snapshot | Invalidation),
  DifferentialSubscriberStatus, and internal DifferentialSubscriber and
  DifferentialRegistry.
- Add DifferentialRegistry to Connection with AtomicU64 counters for
  snapshot_bootstraps_total and fallback_invalidations_total, and a
  Mutex<HashMap> of active subscribers keyed by subscriber_id.
- Implement register_differential_view_subscriber: validates that
  PRAGMA fsqlite_differential_views = ON and autocommit mode, executes
  the view's SELECT at the current MVCC snapshot, sends a Snapshot
  event with the bootstrap rows, and registers the subscriber to
  receive Invalidation events starting at snapshot_seq + 1.
- Implement unregister_differential_subscriber and accessors
  differential_subscribers / differential_subscriber_count.
- Wire emit_differential_commit_invalidations into both commit paths
  (normal commit in execute_write_transaction and the cleanup commit
  path): iterates active subscribers whose stream_begin_seq <= the
  committed seq, sends Invalidation events via mpsc, and auto-removes
  subscribers whose receivers have been dropped.
- Tighten the existing capture_time_travel_snapshot call sites: the old
  code extracted committed_seq via .map(|s| s.get()).unwrap_or(0) even
  when no commit happened; now both sites use if-let to only snapshot
  and emit invalidations when last_local_commit_seq is Some.
- Add lookup_differential_view helper and differential_status_rows for
  the new PRAGMA fsqlite.differential_status (also accessible as
  differential_status / fsqlite_differential_status), which reports
  enabled, active_subscriptions, snapshot/invalidation totals,
  latest_visible_commit_seq, and active_views.

IN-subquery view resolution fix:
- Add in_subquery_references_view: checks whether the subquery's FROM
  clause (source table or any join) names a view that exists in
  conn.views but not in conn.schema (i.e., an unmaterialized view).
- Change expr_contains_rewritable_subquery to always mark IN subqueries
  as rewritable (InSet::Subquery(_) => true) so the rewrite pass can
  inspect them.  Previously, only subqueries matching
  in_subquery_needs_eager_eval were marked, causing view-referencing
  IN subqueries to be skipped entirely and silently emit NULL via VDBE
  codegen.
- Update rewrite_in_expr to: (a) skip correlated IN subqueries (which
  reference outer table columns and must be evaluated per-row), and
  (b) add in_subquery_references_view(sub, conn) as a third trigger
  for eager evaluation alongside the existing rewrite_in_subqueries
  flag and in_subquery_needs_eager_eval check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
